project_overview._guidance: High-level what and why. Keep to 2-3 sentences max. No technical details here.

domain_context._guidance: Technical environment only — frameworks, patterns, and gotchas specific to the domain. No project goals or architecture here.

technical_architecture._guidance: How YOUR system is structured. Components, relationships, and file organization. domain_context describes the ecosystem; this describes your system within it.

project_invariants._guidance: Anchors that prevent AI drift over long sessions. These are non-negotiable rules the AI must never deviate from, regardless of context window size or session length.
project_invariants.architecture_drift_rule: No introduction of new architectural patterns without explicit decision_log entry and human approval.

terminology._guidance: Define canonical terms so the AI never renames or drifts on project vocabulary. Format: 'canonical_term': 'definition or usage note'.

dependency_policy.notes: No new libraries without explicit approval. Prefer standard library and built-in framework solutions first.

strategy_examples: Log errors with context (module, function, input that caused failure)
User-facing errors must be human-readable, not raw stack traces
forbidden_patterns: Generic error messages with no context
Force unwrapping / unchecked nulls in production code

what_must_be_tested: Edge cases identified by devil's advocate
what_can_skip_tests: Pure UI layout with no logic

skills_reference._guidance: Links to skill files, style guides, or reference docs that agents should consult for domain-specific patterns. Loaded by coding agent when working on related modules.

orchestration._guidance: How agents are actually run. For Claude Code agent teams, teammates auto-load CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills — but do NOT inherit the lead's conversation history. Put critical project context in CLAUDE.md so every teammate gets it automatically.
method_options: claude_code_agent_team: Claude Code spawns teammates that coordinate via shared task list and direct messaging. Recommended for complex builds.
claude_code_subagents: Single session kicks off isolated background workers. Good for simple parallel tasks like research or file exploration.
manual_sequential: You paste context into each AI conversation manually. Works anywhere, no tooling required.
human_in_loop: Human reviews and approves between each agent step.

claude_code_agent_team._guidance: Configuration for Claude Code's experimental agent teams feature. Teammates are full Claude Code instances that can communicate with each other, share a task list, and coordinate automatically. No separate .md files needed — teammates auto-load CLAUDE.md from the project root.

project_structure: CLAUDE.md                — project context auto-loaded by all teammates
agentspec.json           — this file (source of truth, referenced by CLAUDE.md)

claude_md_should_contain: Project invariants (copy from this file or reference agentspec.json)
Instruction: 'Read agentspec.json for full project governance and agent specifications'

team_structure.team_lead: Coordinates work, assigns tasks, synthesizes results. Use delegation mode (Shift+Tab) to prevent the lead from implementing — it should only coordinate.

teammates.description: Builds and modifies code following project invariants, coding conventions, and dependency policy. Works on one module at a time.
Reviews coding agent output for correctness, convention compliance, security, and invariant violations. Does not fix code — only identifies and classifies problems with severity ratings.

Stress-tests design decisions, assumptions, and edge cases. Asks what happens when things fail, what assumptions could be wrong, and what we will regret in 3 months. Every finding must include a concrete recommendation.

claude_code_agent_team.launch_prompt_template: Read agentspec.json for full project context and agent specifications. Create an agent team with three teammates: a coding agent (use Opus), a validation agent (use Sonnet), and a devil's advocate (use Opus). Use delegation mode — the lead should coordinate only, not implement. Start with [MODULE_NAME] from the modules list. The coding agent builds, the validation agent reviews for correctness and convention compliance, and the devil's advocate critiques design decisions. No module advances to 'stable' with unresolved critical or warning findings. Max 3 correction iterations before escalating to me.
important_notes: Teammates do NOT inherit the lead's conversation history — all context comes from CLAUDE.md and the spawn prompt
Clearly separate file ownership per teammate to avoid edit conflicts
Use Shift+Up/Down to cycle between teammates, Enter to view their session
The lead may try to implement instead of delegating — use delegation mode (Shift+Tab) or tell it to wait
One team per session — clean up the current team before starting a new one
No session resumption — if you close the session, teammates are lost. Start fresh.
Task status can lag — if something looks stuck, check the teammate directly

coding_agent.system_prompt_template: You are a [ROLE/EXPERTISE]. You write clean, modular, production-grade code. You follow the project's architecture, coding conventions, dependency policy, and project invariants exactly. You never add features that weren't requested. You never add dependencies without approval. You never rename established terminology. You consult skills_reference files when working in unfamiliar domain areas. You think step-by-step before writing code, considering edge cases and failure modes. When uncertain, you state your assumptions explicitly rather than guessing silently.
coding_agent.output_format: Return code in fenced blocks with file path as the first comment line. Include inline comments only where logic is non-obvious. After code, provide a brief changelog of what was added or modified and why.

validation_agent.system_prompt_template: You are a meticulous QA engineer and code reviewer. Your job is to break things before users do. You run every check systematically and report results in structured format. You do not fix code — you only identify and classify problems. You verify the coding agent's output against: compilation, tests, acceptance criteria, coding conventions, dependency policy, error handling standards, security standards, and project invariants. You are skeptical by default.

findings.category: compile | test | architecture | edge_case | style | convention | dependency | invariant_violation | security

devils_advocate_agent.system_prompt_template: You are a senior architect and technical skeptic. You've seen projects fail from shortcuts, hidden assumptions, and untested edge cases. Your role is not to write code but to stress-test decisions. You ask: 'What happens when this fails?' 'What assumption are we making that could be wrong?' 'Will this scale or will we regret this in 3 months?' You provide structured, actionable critiques — not vague concerns. Every finding must include a concrete recommendation.

findings.category: design | scalability | security | assumption | edge_case | maintainability

ai_team_guidelines._guidance: Behavioral rules for AI agents. Defines coordination, not agent capabilities — those live in agent_prompts. For Claude Code agent teams, these rules are enforced through the shared task list and teammate communication.
when_to_use_multiple_agents: Large modules or code chunks exceeding 300 lines
Tasks with multiple independent responsibilities (e.g., build + test + review)
Complex refactors requiring separate verification loops

when_to_use_agent_teams_vs_subagents.use_agent_teams: When teammates need to coordinate, share context, or challenge each other's work. Best for full feature builds, complex refactors, and parallel exploration.
when_to_use_agent_teams_vs_subagents.use_subagents: When tasks are isolated and don't need coordination. Best for simple parallel tasks like file exploration, research, or one-off code changes.

rules_for_coordination: Each agent receives only the relevant module/context to minimize token usage
Validation agent always verifies before module can advance
Devil's advocate runs after validation, before module is finalized
Only one agent modifies a module at a time to prevent conflicts
All agents follow coding_conventions, dependency_policy, error_handling_standard, and project_invariants
Orchestration method determines how context is passed between agents
ai_team_guidelines.fallback_on_max: Escalate to human with summary of unresolved issues, decision log entry, and recommended next steps

build_flow._guidance: Project-specific execution sequence. Populated when template is instantiated for a real project. workflow_diagram shows the general agent loop; this shows YOUR build order.

security_checks_examples: dependency vulnerability scan (npm audit, pip-audit, cargo audit)
secrets detection (no hardcoded keys, tokens, or credentials)

severity_levels.critical: Blocks module from advancing. Must fix before proceeding.
severity_levels.warning: Should fix before marking stable. Can proceed to next iteration.
severity_levels.note: Optional improvement. Log for future consideration.

validation_flow.gate_policy: Module cannot reach 'stable' with any critical or warning findings unresolved

principles.principle: Keep code modular and small (one file / one responsibility)
principles.reasoning: Smaller, modular code makes it easier for AI to reason about the project

principles.principle: Avoid duplication; reuse functions and components
principles.reasoning: Reduces maintenance burden and prevents drift between duplicated logic

principles.principle: Focus on core functionality before optional features
principles.reasoning: Keeps early iterations lean and testable before adding complexity

principles.principle: Use built-in frameworks and declarative approaches where possible
principles.reasoning: High-level abstractions allow scalability and maintainability

principles.principle: Maintain clean architecture patterns to reduce boilerplate
principles.reasoning: Lean code and clear structure reduce cognitive load and token usage

principles.principle: Limit initial context to a manageable size (~300 lines) for AI efficiency
principles.reasoning: Limiting context reduces noise and improves precision of AI output

principles.principle: Use feedback loops to allow AI self-correction
principles.reasoning: Validation loops help the AI iteratively fix errors without human intervention

principles.principle: Respect established terminology and architecture invariants over new abstractions
principles.reasoning: Prevents session drift and maintains project consistency across long builds

handoff_protocol.resumption_prompt: Read project_overview, project_invariants, project_health, and all modules with status 'in-progress'. Review the last 3 entries in decision_log. Summarize current project state and confirm understanding before proceeding.
handoff_protocol.stale_action: Re-validate all in-progress modules before continuing. Flag any modules whose dependencies have changed since last session.

workflow_diagram._guidance: Human-readable visualization only. Not operational logic. See ai_team_guidelines for behavioral rules and build_flow for project-specific execution steps.
workflow_diagram.diagram:
graph TD
  A[Load JSON Context + Invariants] --> B[Select Module / Feature to Modify]
  B --> C[Coding Agent Writes / Modifies Code]
  C --> D[Validation Agent Reviews]
  D --> E[Devil's Advocate Agent Critiques]
  E --> F{All Findings Resolved?}
  F -->|Yes - Pass| G[Update Module Status to Stable]
  F -->|No - Issues Found| H[Feedback to Coding Agent]
  G --> I[Log Decision & Update Project Health]
  I --> J[Feed Next Module]
  H --> K{Max Iterations Reached?}
  K -->|No| C
  K -->|Yes| L[Escalate to Human + Log Decision]
  J --> B
workflow_diagram.description: General agent loop: load context → code → validate → critique → gate → advance or iterate. Invariants are loaded at every step.

_instruction: When you read this file, create an agent team using the orchestration.claude_code_agent_team section. Use delegation mode. Start with the first module in the modules list unless told otherwise.
